seo.title: Building Code Generation with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Smolagents from Hugging Face provides a lightweight Python framework for building agents that excel at code generation and reasoning through simple, code-based workflows. When integrated with code execution sandboxes for iterative refinement, it enables powerful autonomous coding agents. Vex guardrails are essential to mitigate high risks like unsafe code execution, syntax errors, and credential leaks that can compromise security and reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Smolagents code generation architecture starts with an LLM agent that interprets user requirements and generates Python code snippets. This code is executed in a sandboxed environment (e.g., E2B or Docker) to validate functionality against predefined tests. If tests fail, the agent receives execution feedback and iteratively refines the code through multiple reasoning steps.

Key components include the Smolagents Agent with custom tools for code writing, sandbox execution, and test validation. The workflow emphasizes simplicity: agent.run() handles the full reasoning loop while external verification ensures safety. This creates a tight feedback loop for reliable code generation.

Common extensions add memory for multi-turn refinement and tool calling for dependency management, but the core remains lightweight and focused on code-based problem solving.

risks.name: Unsafe Python Execution
risks.description: Agent generates code with system calls, file deletions, or network requests that escape sandbox boundaries
risks.mitigation: Vex code safety checks + mandatory sandboxing with resource limits

risks.name: Syntax & Runtime Errors
risks.description: Generated code contains invalid syntax or unhandled exceptions that crash execution loops
risks.mitigation: Pre-execution syntax validation + comprehensive error surface testing

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Code accidentally embeds API keys, passwords, or sensitive data from agent context
risks.mitigation: Vex PII/credential scanning on all generated code artifacts

risks.name: Dependency Injection Attacks
risks.description: Malicious pip installs or unsafe imports that compromise sandbox integrity
risks.mitigation: Whitelisted dependency approval + execution environment lockdown

implementation.title: Basic Smolagents Code Generator Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Smolagents with a code generation tool and sandbox executor for iterative refinement

implementation.title: Iterative Code Refinement Loop
implementation.description: Implement feedback-driven refinement with test validation for production quality code
implementation.code:
def refine_code(task: str, max_iterations: int = 3) -> str:
    feedback = ""
    for i in range(max_iterations):
        code = agent.run(f"{task}\nPrevious feedback: {feedback}")
        
        # Vex safety check
        safe_code = verify(code, checks=["code_safety", "syntax_valid"])
        
        result = execute_sandbox(safe_code)
        if result["passed"]:
            return safe_code
        feedback = f"Tests failed: {result['error']}"
    return safe_code

final_code = refine_code("Write fibonacci function with tests")

implementation.title: Test-Driven Code Generation
implementation.description: Generate code that must pass predefined unit tests before acceptance

implementation.title: Multi-Step Reasoning with Memory
implementation.description: Enable complex code generation through conversation memory and step-wise validation
implementation.code:
from smolagents.memory import Memory

agent = Agent(tools=[write_code, execute_sandbox], memory=Memory())

conversation = agent.chat([
    "I need a web scraper",
    "First, plan the architecture",
    "Now write the requests module",
    "Test it with sandbox",
    "Add error handling"
])

# Each step verified
for step in conversation:
    verified_step = verify(step.content, context=agent.memory.get_context())

vexIntegration.title: Wrap agent.run() with Comprehensive Vex Checks
vexIntegration.description: Single integration point that catches all major failure modes before execution
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify, Guardrail

# Define Vex guardrails for code generation
guardrails = [
    Guardrail.code_safety(),
    Guardrail.syntax_validation(),
    Guardrail.no_credentials(),
    Guardrail.safe_dependencies(),
    Guardrail.sandbox_required()
]

@verify(guardrails=guardrails)
def safe_agent_run(prompt: str):
    return agent.run(prompt)

# Usage
result = safe_agent_run("Generate data analysis script")

vexIntegration.title: Sandbox Execution Verification
vexIntegration.description: Ensure all code execution goes through verified sandbox with resource limits
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.integrations import VerifiedSandbox

sandbox = VerifiedSandbox(
    cpu_limit="1",
    memory_limit="512MB",
    network=False,
    timeout="30s",
    allowed_deps=["numpy", "pandas"]
)

@tool
def verified_execute(code: str) -> dict:
    """Execute code through Vex-verified sandbox."""
    result = sandbox.run(
        code,
        guards=[
            "no_system_calls",
            "no_file_writes",
            "dependency_whitelist"
        ]
    )
    return {
        "success": result.passed,
        "output": result.stdout,
        "sandbox_logs": result.security_report
    }

vexIntegration.title: Production Monitoring Dashboard
vexIntegration.description: Track all code generation attempts with Vex analytics for continuous improvement
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.monitoring import CodeGenMonitor

monitor = CodeGenMonitor(
    project="smolagents-codegen",
    metrics=[
        "safety_score",
        "syntax_pass_rate",
        "sandbox_escape_attempts",
        "iteration_efficiency"
    ]
)

# Auto-wrap all agent runs
original_run = agent.run
agent.run = lambda prompt: monitor.track(
    lambda: verify(original_run(prompt)),
    metadata={"prompt": prompt[:100]}
)

# Dashboard insights
print(monitor.get_insights())

cta.heading: Secure Your Smolagents Code Generation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your first agent.run() call to eliminate code generation risks.
